# terrible_community_postgresql_postgresql_publication (Data Source)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this data source against
- `name` (String) Name of the publication to add, update, or remove.

### Optional

- `cascade` (Boolean) Drop publication dependencies. Has effect with *state=absent* only.
- `columns` (String) List of tables and its columns to add to the publication. If no columns are passed for table, it will be published as a whole. Mutually exclusive with *tables* and *tables_in_schema*.
- `comment` (String) Sets a comment on the publication. To reset the comment, pass an empty string.
- `login_db` (String) Name of the database to connect to and where the publication state will be changed. The `db` alias is deprecated and will be removed in version 5.0.0.
- `owner` (String) Publication owner. If *owner* is not defined, the owner will be set as *login_user* or *session_role*.
- `parameters` (String) Dictionary with optional publication parameters. Available parameters depend on PostgreSQL version.
- `rowfilters` (String) Optional dictionary of row filters to apply to *tables* or *columns* of the publication. Mutually exclusive with *tables_in_schema*.
- `session_role` (String) Switch to session_role after connecting. The specified session_role must be a role that the current login_user is a member of. Permissions checking for SQL commands is carried out as though the session_role were the one that had logged in originally.
- `state` (String) The publication state.
- `tables` (String) List of tables to add to the publication. If no value is set all tables are targeted. If the publication already exists for specific tables and *tables* is not passed, nothing will be changed. If you need to add all tables to the publication with the same name, drop existent and create new without passing *tables*. Mutually exclusive with *tables_in_schema* and *columns*.
- `tables_in_schema` (String) Specifies a list of schemas to add to the publication to replicate changes for all tables in those schemas. If you want to remove all schemas, explicitly pass an empty list `[]`. Supported since PostgreSQL 15. Mutually exclusive with *tables* and *columns*.
- `trust_input` (Boolean) If `false`, check whether values of parameters *name*, *tables*, *owner*, *session_role*, *params* are potentially dangerous. It makes sense to use `false` only when SQL injections via the parameters are possible.

### Read-Only

- `alltables` (Boolean) Flag indicates that all tables are published.
- `exists` (Boolean) Flag indicates the publication exists or not at the end of runtime.
- `queries` (String) List of executed queries.
